name: Fudge # 3
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Firmly Packed Light Brown Sugar
2 cups White Sugar (or All White Sugar)
recipeInstructions:
Old Method:
1. Stir constantly while the mixture is boiling.
2. Add vanilla flavoring (1 teaspoon).
3. Turn off the heat and stir until it is nearly hard before pouring into a buttered pan.
Modern Method:
1. Butter the sides of a 3-quart saucepan and prewarm the candy thermometer.
2. Use the same ingredients as above, but substitute 1 cup Half and Half for the milk.
3. Place the mixture in the saucepan; stir constantly until the sugar is dissolved and it reaches a boil.
4. Reduce heat while maintaining the boil and continue cooking, stirring infrequently if necessary.
5. When the mixture reaches the soft ball stage (234 to 240°F / 112 to 115.5°C), turn off the heat and add vanilla flavoring (1 teaspoon).
6. Allow the fudge to cool; avoid shocking it by placing the pan in a large square pan filled with ice or cold water.
7. When the fudge has cooled on top, check its consistency by holding your hand over the candy.
8. Gently stir and pour into a buttered 8 x 8-inch pan.
- SERVES: 4 (or one chocoholic)
